zh.Donington, Lincolnshire, England: 英國林可爾史爾多寧頓
zh.Donnington, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾堂寧頓 白 維林頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Winter於大約1535年出生在英國史洛普爾多林頓時，她的父親Hugh Winter27歲，母親Sarah Stewart23歲。她於1560年與Humphrey Wheelock在英國史洛普爾多林頓結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1595年享年61歲。

zh-hans.Donnington, Shropshire, England: 英国史洛普爾堂寧頓 白 維林頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Winter于大约1535年出生在英国史洛普爾多林頓时，她的父亲Hugh Winter27岁，母亲Sarah Stewart23岁。她于1560年与Humphrey Wheelock在英国史洛普爾多林頓结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1595年享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Winter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English (Old English) personal name Winter (Old English Wintra), originally a nickname from the word for ‘winter’ and perhaps still a nickname (see 2 below) in the period of surname formation.
English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name from Middle English winter, a variant of Middle English, Anglo-Norman French viniter, vinter ‘wine merchant’.
Irish: Anglicized form (part translation) of Gaelic Mac Giolla-Gheimhridh ‘son of the lad of winter’, from geimhreadh ‘winter’. This name is also Anglicized McAlivery.

variants.name: Winters
meanings: Dutch and German: patronymic from   Winter  .
English: variant of Winter , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish: adopted for Mac Giolla Gheimhridh ‘son of the lad of winter’, from geimhreadh ‘winter’. Compare Winter 4.

variants.name: Winther
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Niels, Aksel, Anders, Bent, Jorgen, Lars, Nels, Sven.  German Gerhardt, Kurt, Otto.
German, Danish, and Swedish: variant of   Winter  .

variants.name: Winterberg
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called with Middle High German winter ‘winter’ + berg ‘mountain, hill’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name composed of German Winter + Berg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Winterman
meanings: English (Kent and Middlesex): occupational name or status name denoting a servant of a man named   Winter   1.
English (Kent and Middlesex): alternatively, perhaps a nickname denoting a person with a cold, wintry appearance or disposition.

English (Kent and Middlesex): occasionally perhaps an occupational name for a wine merchant or a wine merchant's servant, from a variant of Middle English and Anglo-Norman French viniter, vinter ‘wine merchant’ (modern English vintner) + man ‘man’.

variants.name: De Winter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Urbain, Marcel.
Dutch; Flemish (also Dewinter): variant of Winter + definite article de, particularly as a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or tavern named with the season.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Winter was born about 1535, in Dorrington, Shropshire, England, her father, Hugh Winter, was 27 and her mother, Sarah Stewart, was 23. She married Humphrey Wheelock in 1560, in Dorrington, Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1595, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Winter est née environ 1535, à Dorrington, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Hugh Winter, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Sarah Stewart, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Humphrey Wheelock en 1560, à Dorrington, Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1595, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Winter um 1535 in Dorrington, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh Winter 27 und ihre Mutter Sarah Stewart 23. Sie hat 1560 in Dorrington, Shropshire, England Humphrey Wheelock geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1595 im Alter von 61 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Winter è nata il circa 1535 nel Dorrington, Shropshire, England, sua padre, Hugh Winter, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Sarah Stewart, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Humphrey Wheelock nel 1560, nel Dorrington, Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1595, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Winterが約1535年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアドーリントンで生まれた時，父Hugh Winterは27歳，母Sarah Stewartは23歳であった。1560年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアドーリントンでHumphrey Wheelockと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1595年に61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Winter(이)가 대략 1535년 영국 슈럽실르 도링턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Winter(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sarah Stewart(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1560년 영국 슈럽실르 도링턴에서 Humphrey Wheelock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1595년 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Winter nasceu aproximadamente 1535, em Dorrington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh Winter, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Stewart, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Humphrey Wheelock em 1560, em Dorrington, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1595, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Winter родилась около 1535 г. в местности доррингтон, шропшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Hugh Winter было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah Stewart, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Humphrey Wheelock в 1560 г. в местности доррингтон, шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1595 г. в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Winter nació aproximadamente 1535, en Dorrington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Hugh Winter, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Sarah Stewart, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Humphrey Wheelock en 1560, en Dorrington, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1595, a la edad de 61 años.
